items.title: Mississauga short-term rental licenses plummet nearly 50% amid fine hikes
items.summary: 273 licensed short-term-rental hosts as of July 14 is down from 523 in July 2025.

items.title: ‘Very high risk’: Peel Region issues air quality warning as levels hit 10+
items.summary: Mississauga, Brampton, Caledon residents urged to monitor AQHI readings, reduce outdoor activities and take precautions amid deteriorating air quality and hot weather.

items.title: VIDEO: 'Largest' meth seizure in Halton police history part of $5M drug bust that led to charges for pair of Peel men
items.summary: As a result of the investigation, 20 kilograms of cocaine and 273 kilograms of methamphetamine were seized, according to police.

items.title: City closes outdoor pools and adjusts recreation programs due to poor air quality
items.summary: Due to wildfires in other parts of Ontario, Mississauga continues to experience poor air quality and a Very High (10+) Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) for the second consecutive day. Based on this rating, residents are advised to avoid strenuous outdoor activities. If you must be outdoors, it is advised to minimize smoke exposure as much as possible, and prioritize keeping cool. Children, older adults, and individuals experiencing symptoms such as coughing or throat irritation should take extra precautions and limit outdoor physical exertion.

items.title: Mississauga woman faces murder charge after 2024 fatal stabbing in Richmond Hill: York police
items.summary: The woman became a suspect after a 16-year-old boy from Montreal died in a hospital after suffering a stab wound, say York police.

items.title: Mississauga’s air quality affected by wildfire smoke
items.summary: Smoke from forest fires is expected to impact air quality in Mississauga, with conditions that may persist through the day. As smoke levels rise, so do health risks — making it important for residents to stay informed and take steps to protect themselves. Wildfire smoke can travel long distances and differs from typical urban air pollution. It contains fine particles and harmful gases that can enter the lungs and bloodstream, potentially causing inflammation and affecting overall health.
